attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway rider who uses climbs as launchpads, bridging solo from early breaks on ascents and dropping companions on climbs to force prolonged chases before finales.
team_tactics: Team provides dual-purpose support: domestiques work within breakaways to enable classification point collection while Van Hecke pushes tempo or attacks on climbs to maximize group survival time.
terrain_preference: Medium-difficulty climbs where he can separate from breakaway companions and force peloton work; struggles on penultimate/final climbs when fresh peloton applies sustained power.
vulnerability: Breakaway efforts collapse when peloton applies organized chase on harder climbs (penultimate climb onwards), suggesting limits against sustained high-tempo climbing from fresher rivals.
key_patterns: Targets breakaways with dual classification objectives: intermediate sprints and KOM points (2 races)
Uses climbs aggressively within breakaways to drop companions and extend solo efforts (2 observations)
Breakaway collapses typically occur on penultimate/final climbs under peloton pressure (2 instances)
Team commits domestiques to breakaways to support his classification hunting efforts (2 races)
form_trajectory: positive: 2019 shows evolution from pure breakaway survival to strategic classification hunting with coordinated team support
